result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: To reconstruct the sequence of sibling-merge operations, perform a post-order traversal of the final tree. For each node u in the final tree, identify its children v1, v2, ..., vk. These children correspond to a subset of the original siblings of u in the initial tree. Let the original set of children of u in the initial tree be S = {s1, s2, ..., sm}. To transform S into the set {v1, v2, ..., vk}, identify the indices in S that correspond to the nodes in the final tree. For every group of adjacent siblings in the initial tree that are not present in the final tree, or for groups that must be merged to form a single child in the final tree, perform sequential merges. Specifically, if a group of siblings {si, si+1, ..., sj} in the initial tree is represented by a single child v in the final tree, output the merges (u, si, si+1), (u, new_sibling, si+2), ... until only one node remains. By processing nodes in post-order, we ensure that all internal merges within the subtrees of the children are completed before we perform merges at the current level u. JUSTIFICATION: A post-order traversal ensures that all merges occurring within the subtrees of the children are performed before any merges involving the children themselves as siblings. This maintains the structural integrity of the tree during the transformation process.
